title: Bourbon biscuits
subtitle: Chocolate sandwich biscuits with chocolate buttercream filling
excerpt: Homemade version of the classic British chocolate biscuit: a crunchy, deeply chocolate dough rolled thin and cut into rectangles, sandwiched with a chocolate buttercream filling. The dough contains cocoa and a small amount of golden syrup for colour and snap. Makes sixteen sandwiches.
sourceNotes: Chocolate sandwich biscuits are documented in British domestic baking from the late nineteenth century. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: chocolate biscuits and biscuit preparations with sweet chocolate fillings are documented in the chapter on biscuits. The combination of a crunchy cocoa biscuit sandwiched with chocolate buttercream is documented in British domestic cookbooks and household baking manuals from the early twentieth century, available through the Internet Archive.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keep in an airtight tin for up to 5 days. The biscuits soften slightly after sandwiching — this is desirable.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf. Rotate the tray at 7 minutes for even colour. The biscuits are done when the edges are dry; they crisp as they cool

glossaryTerms.definition: Two biscuit layers joined with a filling — usually a buttercream or cream. The biscuits must be fully cooled before sandwiching: warm biscuits make the buttercream melt and slide. Press the sandwich together firmly but gently: enough to bond the filling to both layers without cracking the biscuits. The filling should reach the edges but not overflow them.

content.text: Homemade bourbon biscuits are better than the bought version: more chocolate in the dough, more chocolate in the filling, and a texture that starts crunchy and softens to something better over the first day. Roll the dough thin (3 mm) and do not skip the cooling time before sandwiching.
Make and bake the biscuits, 12–14 minutes

Preheat the oven to 175°C fan. Pulse together the {{plain-flour}} for the biscuit, {{cocoa-powder}} for the biscuit, {{icing-sugar}} for the biscuit, and {{sea-salt-fine}} in a bowl. Rub in the cold {{unsalted-butter}} for the biscuit until the mixture resembles fine crumbs. Add the {{golden-syrup}} and mix until the dough comes together, adding a splash of {{whole-milk}} for the biscuit if needed. Roll to 3 mm thick on a lightly floured surface and cut into rectangles of about 7 x 3 cm. Place on lined baking trays and prick each one with a fork a few times. Bake for 12–14 minutes, rotating the tray at 7 minutes. Cool completely on the tray — they will crisp as they cool.

Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} for the filling until pale. Beat in the sifted {{icing-sugar}} for the filling and {{cocoa-powder}} for the filling until smooth. Add {{whole-milk}} for the filling to reach a spreadable consistency. To make a
: spread a teaspoon of filling on the flat side of one biscuit and press a second biscuit on top, flat side down. Press firmly. Leave to set for 20 minutes before eating.
